sources.notes: Statement and constraints: repeatedly delete the minimum m, multiply answer by m, and insert 1..m-1.
Official solution confirms that after shifting values by one, fully processing value v has block length 2^v and contribution can be precomputed by DP/DFS.
Chinese solution cross-checks the full-block recurrence and binary-block consumption of k.

annotation.summary: Given an initial positive integer multiset S, repeat k times: take the minimum m, multiply it into the answer, delete it, and insert 1..m-1; output the product modulo 1e9+7.
annotation.constraints: sum n <= 200000, k <= 1e9, s_i <= 1e9; only about 30 low value levels can affect the answer.
annotation.core_idea: Rewrite value x as v=x-1. Fully deleting a value v and all smaller values it recursively creates takes 2^v operations, with contribution dp[0]=1 and dp[v]=(v+1)*prod_{j<v}dp[j]. Sort the initial multiset. From smaller values upward, consume k: complete blocks use dp[v] directly; the first incomplete block is solved by a recursive dfs(v,k) that unfolds subblocks j=0..v-1 according to the binary capacity left. Since k<=1e9, values above about 30 are truncated to a single partial block.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n + log^2 k) per test case after small precomputation
tricks: A full value-v block has length 2^v.
Precompute full deletion contributions by DP.
Use the binary structure of k to handle a partial block.
Large values can be capped to the range that k can reach.

solution_variants.name: power-block-dp-recursion
solution_variants.summary: Sort the initial multiset, multiply complete blocks using dp[v], and solve the first incomplete block with dfs(v,k) over smaller power-of-two subblocks.

tags.evidence: The contribution of fully processing a value v is recursively determined by all smaller full-block contributions.
Operation counts decompose into 2^v blocks, and partial-block processing consumes k by binary-scale subblocks.
Because k<=1e9, only about 30 levels need explicit handling despite values up to 1e9.
The initial multiset must be processed in increasing minimum-value order.
